title: What to Look Out for in Economic Data This Week (May 4-8)
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Key economic data this week includes labor market reports, S&P Services PMI, and NY Fed inflation expectations. Focus on April jobs report and productivity/unit
excerpt: Key economic data this week includes labor market reports, S&P Services PMI, and NY Fed inflation expectations. Focus on April jobs report and productivity/unit labor costs. Watch for Fed officials' comments post-April Fed meeting.

content.introduction: Key economic data this week includes labor market reports, S&P Services PMI, and NY Fed inflation expectations. Focus on April jobs report and productivity/unit labor costs. Watch for Fed officials' comments post-April Fed meeting.
body: By [Karee Venema](https://www.kiplinger.com/author/karee-venema). in [News](https://www.kiplinger.com/news). * [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.kiplinger.com%2Finvesting%2Feconomy%2Fthis-weeks-economic-calendar). Incoming [jobs](https://www.kiplinger.com/economic-forecasts/jobs) data is the major theme of this week's economic calendar, starting with hiring and firing numbers for March on Tuesday, continuing with initial jobless claims on Wednesday and ending with the April jobs report on Friday. In the immediate aftermath of the [April Fed meeting](https://www.kiplinger.com/news/live/fed-meeting-updates-and-commentary-april-2026) and Jerome Powell's final press conference as Fed chair, we'll hear from New York Fed President John Williams on Monday and Thursday and Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee on Wednesday. [Sign up](https://www.kiplinger.com/newsletter). "We estimate that nonfarm payroll employment was flat in April," a team of Barclays economists led by [Marc Giannoni](https://www.linkedin.com/in/marcpgiannoni/) writes, "following the sharp swings in January (+160k), February (-133k) and March (+178k).". * [Kiplinger's Earnings Calendar](https://www.kiplinger.com/investing/stocks/17494/next-week-earnings-calendar-stocks). * [Kiplinger's Economic Forecasts](https://www.kiplinger.com/economic-forecasts). * [Stock Market Holidays in 2026](https://www.kiplinger.com/investing/stock-market-holidays). * [Stock Market Trading Hours](https://www.kiplinger.com/investing/602886/stock-market-trading-hours). [Karee Venema](https://www.kiplinger.com/author/karee-venema). * [Subscriptions Store](https://www.kiplinger.com/subscription). * [Kiplinger Newsletters](https://www.kiplinger.com/newsletter). * [About Kiplinger](https://www.kiplinger.com/about-us). * [Stock Market Today](https://www.kiplinger.com/tag/closing-bell). * [Economic Forecasts](https://www.kiplinger.com/economic-forecasts). * [Tools and Calculators](https://www.kiplinger.com/tools).

**US S&P Services PMI April Final** The S&P Global US Services PMI surveys purchasing managers across the services sector on business activity, new orders, employment, and prices. In the previous report (March Final), the Services PMI was revised down to 49.8 from 51.7 in February, marking the first contraction in over three years. If the final April PMI is revised higher (confirming the preliminary rebound), equities, especially consumer-facing and service-driven sectors, may benefit as growth concerns ease. A stronger final reading would likely support the dollar, as it reinforces confidence in US economic resilience. If services activity is revised higher, yields may rise, reflecting firmer growth expectations. If the final reading disappoints, yields may fall as investors price in softer activity and increased odds of policy easing. A rebound in services, particularly if paired with firm price pressures, would support a higher-for-longer stance. If weakness persists in the final data, especially in demand and employment, it would strengthen the case for a more accommodative policy path as growth risks increase.

4 min read  Labor Market. The week ahead is chock-full of labor market indicators. Big names scheduled to report this week include Palantir, AMD, McDonald's, and Arm. The industry analysts' consensus forecast for S&P 500 companies’ aggregate earnings growth in 2026 has climbed to 19.8%, well above the 11.7% and 13.6% figures posted in 2024 and 2025 (chart). With that said, let's take a look at the key releases most likely to shape investors' thinking on business activity, the state of the consumer, the labor market, and inflation this week:. **(4) Productivity and unit labor costs.** Q1-2026 nonfarm productivity (Thu) should show a weak increase close to the 1.8% q/q (saar) gain during Q4-2025 (chart). **(5) NY Fed inflation expectations.** The NY Fed's Inflation Expectations release (Thu) for April is likely to show a jump in the year-ahead measure closer to 4.0%, up from 3.4% in March (chart).
